data.narrative:
Oklo is a public-stage nuclear energy company founded by Jacob DeWitte and Caroline Cochran, currently valued at $10B+ after raising $500M+. The nuclear energy sector comprises a modest comp universe of 11 peers total—3 public companies (including Oklo itself, NNE, and SMR) and 8 private firms spanning advanced reactor technologies. The private peer set includes Nano Nuclear Energy, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Zap Energy.

Peer-median valuation methodology is not applicable here. The nuclear energy sector lacks sufficient same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier peers to satisfy minimum triangulation thresholds. While 7 same-sector peers exist, none of the filtered cohorts yielded three or more comparable datapoints necessary for reliable median derivation. Applying an all-sector median would introduce material distortion given the capital intensity and regulatory complexity specific to nuclear technologies.

With peer-median analysis inapplicable and no other quantitative methods provided in the available data, this valuation cannot be triangulated through the standard VC methodology framework. Additional methods—such as precedent transaction analysis, DCF sensitivity modeling, or stage-based funding multiples—would be required to establish a defensible range. The primary uncertainty stems from the sector's sparse public trading history and heterogeneous technology approaches across the private comp set, limiting direct comparability to Oklo's specific advanced reactor pathway.
